headline: The xx Concludes Eight-Year Hiatus with Mexico City Performance, Signaling Renewed Activity
deck: The acclaimed indie-pop trio performed its first concert in eight years, marking a significant re-engagement with live performance and potential new material.
body: The acclaimed indie-pop trio The xx returned to the stage on Friday, April 3, marking their first live performance as a group in eight years. The concert, held at Mexico City’s Pepsi Center WTC, signals a significant re-engagement with their career and potentially a new creative phase, following reports of new music in development since 2023.

This performance concludes an eight-year hiatus from live appearances as a trio, a period that had generated considerable anticipation among their global fanbase. The band had previously characterized this return as initiating a “next chapter” for the group, underscoring the strategic nature of their re-emergence.
During the Mexico City engagement, the ensemble — comprising Romy, Jamie xx, and Oliver Sims — delivered a 19-song set that spanned their collective discography.

The setlist incorporated material from all three of their studio albums: the 2009 debut 'xx', 2012’s 'Coexist', and 2018’s 'I See You'. The performance commenced with “Crystalised” and concluded with “Infinity,” with an encore presenting the inaugural live rendition of “Season’s Run.”

Furthermore, the concert integrated selections from each member’s individual repertoires, a notable inclusion for a group performance. This encompassed Romy’s ”Enjoy Your Life,” Sim’s “GMT,” and Jamie xx’s ”Loud Places,” highlighting the artistic evolution of the trio beyond their collaborative work.

Friday’s performance represented the initial installment of three scheduled shows for The xx in Mexico City. This localized series of engagements precedes a more expansive return to major international festival circuits.

The band is slated for prominent festival appearances, including headlining slots at Lollapalooza, Primavera Sound, and Outside Lands, in addition to a scheduled performance at Coachella. These bookings indicate a substantial re-integration into the global music festival landscape.

Concurrently with these live engagements, reports indicate that the band has been actively developing a follow-up album to 'I See You' since 2023. Further corroboration of ongoing creative activity emerged earlier this year with the release of new studio footage, suggesting a forthcoming release of new material.

The band's sustained influence within contemporary music was formally acknowledged in 2021 when The xx was featured in Pitchfork's 'The 200 Most Important Artists of Pitchfork’s First 25 Years'. This historical recognition contextualizes the significance of their current return to both the studio and the stage.
sources: Here’s What Happened at the xx’s First Show in Eight Years
